3.1: Each Umamusume has different goals.\nThis is your goal for the time being!
3.2: These commands let you progress through the Career.
3.3: Executing a command consumes a turn.
3.5: These are the different types of Training!\nEach one increases different attributes.
3.9: Take note of the Failure Rate.
3.10: The lower your trainee's Energy, the higher the Failure Rate.
3.11: Failing a Training can lead to drops in attributes\nand Mood, so be careful!
3.13: When Energy is low, have your trainee rest to recover it.
3.15: Next, select a skill to learn. Use the skill points you obtained earlier!
3.18: You're all set! Time to enter a race!
3.19: The races you are able to participate in are shown in this list.
3.22: Predictions of a runner's performance are denoted\nby the symbols [△] [▲] [○] [◎] from lowest to highest.
3.23: The higher a runner's ability and Mood, the higher her popularity and the expectations placed on her will be.
3.24: Of course, popularity doesn't guarantee a win,\nbut the more favored your trainee is, the more you can expect from her!
3.25: You can also change a runner's strategy in the paddock.
3.26: It's best to select a strategy that plays to a runner's strengths.
3.27: You're all set!\nIt's finally time to start the race!
3.28: Congratulations on making it through a Career!
3.29: Don't forget to use up your remaining skill points!
3.30: And with that, the Career playthrough is complete!
3.31: A trainee who has completed her Career will be evaluated based on set criteria\nand then registered as a veteran.
3.32: Good luck! Try to aim for the highest rank!
3.33: A trainee who has completed her Career can obtain Sparks.
3.34: If you select her as a Legacy Umamusume, you will be able to use her as Inspiration for another runner and pass on her Sparks!
3.36: Make sure you praise your trainee for all her hard work!
3.37: Usually, a winning concert is held after a race,\nbut since you're in the middle of practice, we'll skip it for now.
3.39: Although it was short this time,\na Career is usually three years long.
3.40: Please treasure the time you spend together with your trainees!
3.41: The top right symbols represent the expert predictions,\nwhile popularity is representative of fan opinion.

48.113201211: At a random point during the second half of the race, your speed will increase; the effect is stronger if your base Wisdom is at least 1200. (Late Surger/Medium)
48.113202111: If you're in the first half of the pack at a random point during the first half of the Final Leg, your acceleration will increase; in overseas Turf races, the skill will instead immediately activate when you're on any straight during your last spurt. (Medium)
48.113202211: At a random point on a random straight during the second half of the Middle Leg, you speed will slightly increase; if you're in the first half of the pack and the race takes place abroad, you will also get a slight (immediate) speed boost. (Pace Chaser/Medium)
48.113301111: At a random point during the second half of the Middle Leg, your speed will increase. (Pace Chaser)
48.113301211: In races between 2000 and 2500 m, if you're trying to overtake someone during the first quarter of the Final Leg, your acceleration will increase; if you're not trying to overtake, the skill will trigger at a random point of the first quarter instead. (Pace Chaser)
48.113501111: If you're in a last spurt on the Final Straight, your speed will greatly increase; if your mood is Great, you will use a little extra stamina and your speed will increase tremendously. (Late Surger)
48.113501211: Two thirds through the race, you will get a speed boost; then, if you try to overtake someone during the Final Corner, then if you're a good position with 400 m remaining, your speed will increase. (Late Surger/Medium)
48.113501221: If you're in the middle of the pack on any straight during the second half of the race, your speed will increase; then, with 1000 m remaining, you will get a slight speed boost. (Late Surger/Long)
48.113701111: At a random point during the second half of the Opening Leg, your speed will increase; (Oonige/Runaway) your speed will only increase slightly, but your stamina will also recover. (Front Runner)
48.113701211: If you're in the first half of the pack at a random point during the first quarter of the Final Leg, your acceleration will increase; if you're first or second, you will also get a slight speed boost. (Front Runner/2000 m+)
48.114301111: If you're not in the front during a random Middle Leg straight, you will get a speed boost; then, if you're in the middle of the pack during the Final Straight, your speed will slightly increase. (Late Surger)
48.114301211: At a random point on a random corner, you will get a speed boost; in Medium races, your acceleration will also slightly increase. (Medium/Long)

49.22:
2500 Jewels 
SSR Start Dash Exchange Ticket
49.29:
1st Anniv. Celebration 
1500 Jewels + SSR Exchange Ticket
49.65:
2nd Anniv. Celebration

49.67:
2nd Anniv. Celebration 
5000 Jewels + SSR Exchange Ticket
49.87:
New Trainer Support 
★3 pick ticket + 1500 jewels
49.88:
New Trainer Support 
SSR pick ticket + 1500 jewels
49.90:
TV Anime Season 3 Celebration 
★3 pick ticket + 1500 jewels
49.94:
3rd Anniv. Celebration 
SSR pick ticket + 5000 jewels
49.96:
3rd Anniv. Celebration 
SSR pick ticket + 1500 jewels

63.1:
There's never a dull day to be had at Tracen Academy. 
Choose a <color=#ff9900>training scenario</color> and tackle its various events. 
Conquer these scenarios to help your trainee grow!
63.2:
Work together with a trainee in the <color=#ff9900>Twinkle Series</color> 
for 3 years and <color=#ff9900>make their dreams come true</color>! 
The trainees' goals are diverse. Guide many 
different girls to become a great trainer.
63.3:
Each trainee is unique, with their very own attributes, skills, 
and aptitudes. You can train them as many times as you like, 
so why not start with <color=#ff9900>the one that stands out to you</color>.
63.4:
Let your trainee <color=#ff9900>carry on veteran's legacies</color>. Their <color=#ff9900>Legacy 
Traits </color>will increase attributes and aptitudes at the start 
of training, and cause <color=#ff9900>Legacy Events</color> during training to help 
them grow. Veterans won't disappear, so take 
advantage to raise ever stronger trainees!
63.5:
Create a <color=#ff9900>deck</color> of support cards to receive corresponding 
<color=#ff9900>support effects</color> and <color=#ff9900>training events</color> from those characters. 
You can not use cards of the <color=#ff9900>trainee you are training</color>.
63.6:
Support cards have <color=#ff9900>types </color>based on their <color=#ff9900>training specialty</color>. 
The effects they exhibit during training are different.
63.7:
When you raise the Friendship Gauge of a support card during training 
and perform its specialty training, <color=#ff9900>Friendship Training</color> will occur. 
In Friendship Training, your trainee will grow more than usual!
63.8:
Use <color=#ff9900>enhancement items</color> to increase your <color=#ff9900>Potential Lv</color>. Raising 
your Potential Lv will increase the number of skills you can 
acquire during training. Reinforcement items can be obtained 
through training and daily races.
63.9:
Use <color=#ff9900>trainee pieces</color> to <color=#ff9900>uncap</color> them. This will increase the 
basic ability of the trainee. Also, when they reach ★3 
they will receive a <color=#ff9900>victory outfit</color>.
63.10:
Use your <color=#ff9900>Support Pt</color> and <color=#ff9900>Money</color> to <color=#ff9900>increase the Lv</color>. As the Lv 
increases, your support effects will increase, giving you an 
edge in your training. Strengthening items can be obtained 
through training and daily races.
63.11:
Use <color=#ff9900>copies of the same support card</color> to <color=#ff9900>uncap</color>. Uncapping 
increases the upper level limit and you can further 
strengthen the support card's Lvl.
63.12:
Support cards dupes can be converted into <color=#ff9900>Horseshoes</color> 
. These valuable items can be exchanged in the store. 
The first support card you acquire will be stored in 
the support card list, subsequent cards will be displayed 
in the vault and can be converted.
63.13:
When you Complete a Career, your trainee will be saved as a 
<color=#ff9900>Veteran</color>. Once there, she can enter the Team Stadium 
and various races, pass on her legacy in Careers, or be
transferred, upon which she is irrevocably lost.
63.14:
Trainee pieces can be converted into <color=#ff9900>clovers</color>. Clovers are 
valuable and can be exchanged for items in the store. Be 
careful when converting pieces, as they can be used to release 
the trainee you are training and to develop new talents.
63.15: Follow the trainees through the <color=#ff9900>Main Story</color> and earn rewards!
63.16:
Meeting new trainees and increasing their <color=#ff9900>Bond</color> allows 
you to progress through <color=#ff9900>their personal stories</color>. Rewards are 
gained for progressing through the stories.
63.17:
Form a <color=#ff9900>team</color> of Veteran Trainees and compete against 
teams from all over the country in the <color=#ff9900>Team Stadium</color>. 
Your team's <color=#ff9900>CLASS</color> will increase according to its success, 
and each week you will earn rewards according to the 
CLASS you belong to.
63.18:
A <color=#ff9900>high score</color> will be recorded for outstanding race 
results. Each week, the CLASS to which you belong 
will change based on your high score.
63.19:
In the Team Stadium, 5 races with different <color=#ff9900>conditions</color> are 
held at a time. The higher your CLASS, the more trainees
you can enter in each race, up to a maximum of 15. 
Form the strongest team you can and take on others!
63.20:
Form your own <color=#ff9900>team</color> of <color=#ff9900>Veterans</color>. For each of 
the five races with different conditions, choose 
the trainee with the best aptitude. The <color=#ff9900>ace</color> horse 
girl will get <color=#ff9900>1.1 times</color> the score.
63.21:
The more strong trainees you have in your team, the higher 
your <color=#ff9900>team rating</color> will be and the higher your <color=#ff9900>team rank</color> will be. 
The higher your team rank, the more rewards you can earn.
63.22:
In the Team Stadium, you will play against <color=#ff9900>opponents 
with similar team ratings</color>. The higher your team 
rating, the higher your score will be. Form a 
strong team and conquer the Team Stadium!
63.23:
Challenge the Legend Race with your saved trainees. The 
higher you finish, the more rewards you get. Tickets required 
for participation are replenished at 05:00 during the event 
period and when the LEGEND trainee is switched.
63.24:
Take on the Daily Race with a Veteran Trainee. You 
can earn various rewards depending on the races you challenge. 
Each day, you will receive up to the <color=#ff9900>maximum number of tickets</color>.
63.25:
In Practice, you can freely hold races under <color=#ff9900>any conditions 
you want</color>. In addition to the Veterans you have trained, 
you can also run races with <color=#ff9900>other trainers' trainees</color>. 
You can hold practice with up to 18 trainees at once!
63.26:
If you see any trainee you like in the Team Stadium 
or a Room Match, you can register her as your <color=#ff9900>Training 
Partner</color> so that you can run races with her. The Star 
Trainee of the friends you are following will also 
be able to run in the race.
63.27:
You can <color=#ff9900>share</color> your favorite <color=#ff9900>Veteran Trainees</color> with your 
Club and online. Actively register your Practice Partners from 
Club members and friends to enhance your practice!
63.28: Send your trainees out to appear at <color=#ff9900>fun events at racecourses around the country</color>!
63.29: You can <color=#ff9900>send your trained trainees to attend a selected event at a particular racecourse</color>. Each event will provide different <color=#ff9900>rewards upon its conclusion</color>.
63.30:
Join a <color=#ff9900>Club</color> with other trainers from around 
the country. You can interact with other members 
and help each other with training.
63.31:
Members can give each other a <color=#ff9900>variety of items</color>. 
You can also rent each other's <color=#ff9900>Star 
Trainee</color> for inheritance at a discount.
63.32:
<size=26>You can watch live performances of <color=#ff9900>songs</color> you have acquired. 
Choose main and back dancers from obtained trainees 
and acquired costumes to watch the live with your favorite 
combination. Songs marked with <color=#ff9900>"Singing"</color>, are voiced 
by the selected trainee. Songs can be acquired through 
the Main Story and training.</size>
63.33:
Trainer information can be viewed and set here. You can expect
special comments from the active home screen trainee on your
<color=#ff9900>birthday</color>, and some scenario content might change depending on
your <color=#ff9900>gender</color>. (Gender can be changed at any time)
63.34:
You can set up a <color=#ff9900>Star Trainee</color> and <color=#ff9900>support card</color> to 
help your followers in their training. You will earn <color=#ff9900>Friend Pt 
the next day</color> depending on the number of times you do this. 
(This will be given as a gift)
63.35:
You can earn titles through <color=#ff9900>title missions</color> and <color=#ff9900>events</color>. You can 
change the title you have earned at any time.
63.36:
Help the Chairman reach her dream! You can earn <color=#ff9900>trophies and 
rewards</color> when you win graded races during training, or Legend 
Races. In addition, you can earn rewards by completing 
missions to collect the specified number of trophies.
63.37:
You can select the Star Trainee and support 
card of the friends you <color=#ff9900>follow</color> from each list in training. 
You also earn more Friend Pt by helping a <color=#ff9900>mutual follower</color> 
to train. If you don't have many followers, try looking 
in the <color=#ff9900>recommendations</color>.
63.38:
Collect records related to trainees to raise your <color=#ff9900>Index Lv</color>. 
You'll accumulate Index Exp based on the evaluation points 
you've earned through training and the stories you've viewed, 
and you'll earn rewards as your Index Lv increases.
63.39:
Take aim and catch trainee plushies (Paka-Puchi)! 
You have <color=#ff9900>3 attempts</color> to catch at least one. 
Your trainee might even gain a <color=#ff9900>new hint</color>!?
63.40:
Congratulations on your promotion to <color=#ff9900>CLASS 2</color>!
The <color=#ff9900>second team formation slot</color> has been unlocked,
allowing you to field larger teams.
63.41:
Congratulations on your promotion to <color=#ff9900>CLASS 3</color>!
The <color=#ff9900>third team formation slot</color> has been unlocked,
allowing you to field larger teams.
63.42:
In a Career, your main goal is to have your trainee
perform well in her <color=#ff9900>target races</color>. In order to achieve
this, you need to <color=#ff9900>develop your trainee's core abilities</color>
through <color=#ff9900>training</color>!
63.43:
By learning <color=#ff9900>skills</color>, your trainee gains a <color=#ff9900>variety of 
effects</color> in <color=#ff9900>races</color>. The Skill Points needed to acquire 
skills are mainly obtained through races, but can 
also be obtained through some events.
63.44:
If your trainee's <color=#ff9900>energy or motivation is low</color> she will not 
be able to perform at her best in training or races. <color=#ff9900>Resting</color> 
restores energy, and <color=#ff9900>going out</color> boosts motivation. Always pay 
attention to her condition!
63.45:
During training, you may acquire <color=#ff9900>conditions</color> that have a 
negative effect on your trainee's training and physical 
condition. The <color=#ff9900>Infirmary</color> can eliminate such conditions, 
so think about making use of it quickly.
63.46:
You can execute various commands while training your trainee! 
Use each command in a well-balanced manner to make 
your trainee into a great trainee!
63.47:
<size=26>From the latter half of June in Junior class, you can start 
<color=#ff9900>racing</color>. Train your trainee well and aim for <color=#ff9900>first place</color>! In 
races, you gain <color=#ff9900>Skill Points</color> to acquire skills and <color=#ff9900>Fans</color> to run 
in big races, based on your finishing position. You'll also 
earn random items based on your position and margin.</size>
63.48:
Trainees make their first appearance on the racing stage in 
the <color=#ff9900>Debut Race</color>. If they finish first, they can run in 
various races. If they don't, you'll have to try to win one of 
the <color=#ff9900>Maiden Races</color> available!
63.49:
<color=#ff9900>Basic abilities</color> such as speed and stamina, 
gained through training, are linked to <color=#ff9900>the various 
abilities displayed during races</color>. Which you need varies 
greatly with <color=#ff9900>distance</color> and <color=#ff9900>running strategy</color>, so <color=#ff9900>create 
a training menu that suits her</color>!
63.50:
The <color=#ff9900>surface and distance</color> where the races take place differ. 
Your trainee suits different tracks depending on her 
<color=#ff9900>surface and distance aptitudes</color>. Aim for races that are <color=#ff9900>marked 
with a ★</color> in the list, and go for the win!
63.51:
The races are divided into five grades: <color=#ff9900>Pre-Open, Open, G3, 
G2, and G1</color>. Let's start with the <color=#ff9900>Pre-Open, Open</color> to win the 
highest G1 race in the Twinkle Series!
63.52:
You can grow your trainee's <color=#ff9900>basic abilities</color> by 
spending <color=#ff9900>energy</color> to <color=#ff9900>train</color> her. In addition to <color=#ff9900>recovering</color> 
energy, more <color=#ff9900>Skill Points</color> can be gained from the 
Wit training than other training.
63.53:
Training with trainees organized as a <color=#ff9900>support card</color> will 
increase your <color=#ff9900>training efficiency and Friendship Gauge</color>. When the 
Friendship Gauge is full, <color=#ff9900>Friendship Training</color> can occur. You may <color=#ff9900>gain 
various benefits</color> if certain others watch you train as well.
63.54:
When a support card trainee has a <color=#ff9900>! mark</color> on her, grab your 
chance to <color=#ff9900>increase Hint Lv of one of this partner's skills</color> upon 
successfully completing the training session. This will confer 
a <color=#ff9900>discount on the Skill Pt needed to acquire the skill</color>.
63.55:
If you train your trainee when her <color=#ff9900>energy is low</color>, she may 
<color=#ff9900>fail the training</color> and her ability may not improve. There are 
<color=#ff9900>various disadvantages</color> to failing a training session, so always 
keep an eye on her <color=#ff9900>energy and failure rate</color>!
63.56:
By spending <color=#ff9900>Skill Points</color>, you can acquire <color=#ff9900>skills</color> that activate 
during races. There are various conditions to activate skills, 
such as the <color=#ff9900>race distance and running strategy</color>, so try to 
<color=#ff9900>acquire skills that match her aptitudes</color>.
63.57:
The <color=#ff9900>hint Lv</color> of a skill may be increased by the result 
of a <color=#ff9900>Support Card or Trainee Event</color>. The skill Pt you 
<color=#ff9900>consume will be discounted</color> depending on the hint Lv, 
so organize support cards that have the skills you want 
to acquire to get the best deals.
63.58:
There are three main types of skills. 
<color=#ff9900>Unique skills</color> are special skills inherent to a trainee. 
<color=#ff9900>Rare skills</color> are powerful skills obtained from special experiences. 
<color=#ff9900>Normal Skills</color> are skills picked up through diverse situations. 
Some other skills can only be acquired through <color=#ff9900>certain Traits</color>.
63.59:
If you raise the level of your support card to a 
certain level, you will get a <color=#ff9900>support bonus 
that will increase your score in the Team Stadium</color>. 
Let's raise the lvl of many support cards!
63.60:
You can view <color=#ff9900>limited stories</color> and <color=#ff9900>movies</color> that have 
been unlocked through events and campaigns.
63.61:
"The Beauty Dream Cup" is back again this year! Chosen as Muses,
Gentildonna and the others visit a mansion to take photos in their dresses.
However, what awaits them are doors closing on their own, mysterious shrieks...
a series of undeniably suspicious phenomena. Scream? Flee back home?
No, the maidens will—face it head-on.
63.62:
During the event period, <color=#ff9900>train your trainees</color> and earn <color=#ff9900>Event 
Points</color>. By collecting Event Points, you can earn various <color=#ff9900>rewards</color>.
63.63:
Collecting Event Points will allow you to advance through the 
<color=#ff9900>event-only story</color>. By advancing the story and clearing event 
missions, you can earn <color=#ff9900>event limited support cards, rainbow 
fragments, and titles</color>.
63.64:
At the end of training, any <color=#ff9900>event bonus trainees or support 
cards</color> will confer a <color=#ff9900>bonus to the event points gained</color>.
63.65:
You can get <color=#ff9900>Event Pt and Roulette Coins</color> by 
completing event missions. You can also earn 
Roulette Coins from the <color=#ff9900>daily missions</color>.
63.66:
You can play <color=#ff9900>roulette derby</color> using <color=#ff9900>roulette coins</color> 
If you <color=#ff9900>achieve bingo</color>, you can get <color=#ff9900>event limited 
support cards and gold fragments</color>.
63.67:
During the event period, you can earn <color=#ff9900>roulette coins</color> by 
<color=#ff9900>training trainees</color> and completing <color=#ff9900>event missions</color>. Collect 
<color=#ff9900>roulette coins</color> to spin the <color=#ff9900>roulette derby</color> wheel.
63.68:
You can get <color=#ff9900>roulette rewards</color> according to your finishing order 
in the roulette derby. When you get a roulette reward you've 
never gotten before, it's your chance to BINGO!
63.69:
Cells will be filled according to roulette rewards earned. 
When 3 consecutive squares are filled (on any axis), <color=#ff9900>a line is 
completed</color>, and you will receive a <color=#ff9900>BINGO reward</color> based on number 
of lines. Complete <color=#ff9900>8 lines of BINGO</color>to exchange your first 3 sheets.
63.70:
Run in the <color=#ff9900>Aoharu Cup</color>, held the turn after June and December 
end, and aim for victory with your team. Winning will increase 
your <color=#ff9900>team ranking</color>, and your <color=#ff9900>trainee and team members will 
grow</color>. Train a strong trainee to <color=#ff9900>win lots of Aoharu Cup</color>!
63.71:
Put together a team to win the Aoharu Hai! With races in each 
category, the winner will be the one with <color=#ff9900>more wins</color> out of 
five. <color=#ff9900>Winning raises your team ranking</color>, more so the higher 
your opponent's ranking.
63.72:
By using the pieces you have collected, you can 
<color=#ff9900>unlock</color> trainees. Once unlocked, you will be able 
to take charge of them in training.
63.73:
Register 3 trainees to <color=#ff9900>match</color> and battle with <color=#ff9900>other trainers</color> 
in real time! Raise strong trainees and aim for glory!
63.74:
There are two leagues, the <color=#ff9900>Open League</color> and the <color=#ff9900>Graded 
League</color>, and you can choose the one that suits you 
best. Depending on the league, the upper limit of the 
<color=#ff9900>training rank</color> of the Veteran Trainees you can 
register to run in the race will differ.
63.75:
<color=#ff9900>Room Match</color> allows you to race against other trainers 
with your Veteran Trainees <color=#ff9900>Host</color>: Set your own 
conditions for the race <color=#ff9900>Join</color>: Join another trainer's 
race. <color=#ff9900>Watch</color>: Enter an ID to watch the race.
63.76:
The race held will be G1. There are two modes. <color=#ff9900>Simple Mode</color> 
allows you to hold a race right away. <color=#ff9900>Detailed mode</color> allows you 
to choose weather, participation conditions, etc. first. Up to 
<color=#ff9900>3 trainees per person</color> can be entered.
63.77:
<color=#ff9900>The league is made up of three rounds</color>. Starting from Round 2, 
you will be divided into Group A and Group B, and the places 
you advance to will depend on your performance in each round 
and group. Let's move on to the final round and win the <color=#ff9900>winner</color>!
63.78:
Recruit participants for your races from the participation 
list. Trainers close to you can also be recruited using 
the <color=#ff9900>Club and Online Sharing functions</color>. For future reference, 
<color=#ff9900>you can save your favorite races</color>. Have a go organizing 
a race with your friends!
63.80:
In Transfer Requests, you can <color=#ff9901>transfer your trained trainees 
to another trainer's team for rewards</color>. Be aware that once 
you transfer someone, she will be gone and you will not be 
able to get her back.
63.81:
Each team has its own expectations. These not only come with 
<color=#ff9901>different rewards</color>, but <color=#ff9901>the higher the expectations, the more 
luxurious the rewards</color> for transferring your trainee to them.
63.82:
Teams with high expectations will reward a piece of the 
transferred <color=#ff9901>Veteran Trainee</color> as a thank you gift.
63.83:
If you train at a training location where a team member has a 
special icon, "<color=#ff9900>Aoharu Special Training</color>" will occur, and <color=#ff9900>that 
team member's basic abilities will increase</color>. Let's use the 
Aoharu Training to grow our team so we can win the Aoharu Hai!
63.84:
Furthermore, a Special Training session <color=#ff9900>with 2 or more</color> 
team members, will <color=#ff9900>boost your trainee's training 
efficiency</color> as well. <color=#ff9900>Basic abilities and skill points 
gained</color> will also increase <color=#ff9900>the more team members</color> participate.
63.86:
Team members you have had many Special Trainings with 
can have an <color=#ff9900>Aoharu Spirit Explosion</color> once. Their <color=#ff9900>basic 
abilities will increase greatly</color> and <color=#ff9900>your trainee will 
also grow and get Skill Hints</color> The key to success is <color=#ff9900>generating 
many Aoharu Spirit Explosions</color>!
63.87:
You can check the items you currently have. You can also use 
consumable items. Some items can be converted to Money. 
<color=#ff9900>Converting items cannot be undone</color>, so be careful.
63.88:
As team members grow, <color=#ff9900>team strength</color> will increase and you will 
receive gifts from Tazuna. Also, each member's <color=#ff9900>Training Lv 
will increase</color> with their basic ability. <color=#ff9900>The strength of your 
team will lead to the growth of your trainee</color> , so be 
proactive in training your team!
63.89:
The <color=#ff9900>team title</color> is an <color=#ff9900>evaluation of your overall</color> team strength 
and team ranking. <color=#ff9900>The better the team title you have, the more 
your trainee will grow in the ending</color> Let's win Aoharu Hai 
and get the strongest team title!
63.90:
Using <color=#ff9900>Event Boost</color> you can spend twice the TP 
at the start of training to gain double the 
<color=#ff9900>Event Pt</color> and <color=#ff9900>Roulette coins</color> at the end.
63.91:
You can <color=#ff9900>uncap</color> support cards by using <color=#ff9900>duplicates of the same card</color> 
. The level limit will be increased. SR and SSR support cards 
can also be uncapped by using <color=#ff9900>Release Crystals</color>.
63.92:
When you end training in <color=#ff9900>Showtime Mode</color>, your <color=#ff9900>SHOW Gauge</color> will 
rise according to your progress and the difficulty level. You 
will receive <color=#ff9900>additional rewards</color> according to the number of 
times you've filled your SHOW Gauge to MAX.
63.93:
In Showtime Mode, the <color=#ff9900>opponent trainees</color> will become stronger 
in training races. When you see the <color=#ff9900>good ending of your Trainee 
</color> at the chosen difficulty level, the next level will be 
unlocked and can be challenged from next training.
63.94:
You can earn more points by training trainees 
<color=#ff9900>according to their aptitude</color> and by achieving 
the specified <color=#ff9900>Score Bonus</color> conditions.
63.95:
In the <color=#ff9900>Trainer Exam</color>, you can check the <color=#ff9900>usage rate 
of support cards</color> for each test in the ranking!
63.96:
A scenario without individual goals, <color=#ff9900>instead chasing "Result 
Points"</color>. Acquire fixed amounts from each of the 3 years! You 
can freely enter any races during training, and obtained Pts 
depend on the race's grade and finishing position!
63.97:
<color=#ff9900>Once all training objectives are complete</color>, you can challenge 
the <color=#ff9900>Twinkle Star Climax</color>! Emerge victorious over the 3-race 
battle to claim your place as "Twinkle Top Star"!
63.98:
When entering races, you will <color=#ff9900>obtain Shop Coins</color>! Shop 
Coins can be traded for training items in the special 
shop. Training items can increase stats, recover energy, 
boost training growths, and have <color=#ff9900>various other effects 
that are sure to help your training</color>!
63.99:
<color=#ff9900>After each second half of December</color>, when changing years, <color=#ff9900>the 
most outstanding trainee is recognized</color>. Do well in races 
while deepening bonds with your supporters to increase your 
fans! When chosen, your trainee's unique skill will level 
up, in addition to other improvements!
63.100:
Sometimes Rivals will enter races. When <color=#ff9900>entering and winning 
those races</color> your <color=#ff9900>trainee's stats will grow</color>, and she can 
<color=#ff9900>obtain Skill Hints</color>. Grow your trainees more by having them 
fight these tough opponents!
63.101:
In the Twinkle Star Climax, <color=#ff9900>you gain Result Pt based 
on your finishing position</color>, and the <color=#ff9900>trainee with 
the most Pts over 3 races is the winner</color>. Aim for the 
top with your carefully trained trainee!
63.102:
In Practice, you can freely hold races under <color=#ff9900>any conditions 
you want</color>. In addition to the Veteran Trainees you have 
bred, you can also run races with <color=#ff9900>other trainers' trainees</color>. 
You can hold practice with up to 18 trainees at once!
63.103:
If you see any trainee you like in the Team Stadium 
or room match, you can register her as your <color=#ff9900>Training 
Partner</color> so that you can run races with her. The Star 
Trainee of the friends you are following will also 
be able to run in the race.
63.104:
You can <color=#ff9900>share</color> your favorite <color=#ff9900>Veteran Trainee</color> with your 
Club and online. Actively register your Practice Partners from 
Club members and friends to enhance your practice!
63.105:
Save trainees in <color=#ff9900>My Entrants</color> to <color=#ff9900>select them easily</color> during 
registration! You can also save them from the results screen. 
Removing Training Partners will remove them from My Entrants 
as well, so be careful!
63.106:
Save trainees in <color=#ff9900>My Entrants</color> to <color=#ff9900>select them easily</color> during 
registration! You can also save them from the results screen. 
Removing Training Partners will remove them from My Entrants 
as well, so be careful!
63.107:
You can view the <color=#ff9900>Top 100 highest scorers</color> in the 
ranking! The <color=#ff9900>Top 30 trainees</color> can be <color=#ff9900>rented 
without limit</color> in the relevant scenario.
63.108:
<color=#ff9900>Trial Coins</color> are earned for exams, and extra can be earned 
through <color=#ff9900>Score Bonuses</color>, <color=#ff9900>Exam Grades</color>, and <color=#ff9900>Missions</color>.
63.109:
You can increase the <color=#ff9900>Hint Lv</color> of your trainee's skills by 
using <color=#ff9900>upgrade items</color>. When doing so, she will start training 
with her <color=#ff9900>Hint Lv already raised</color> for those skills.
63.110:
Race in the Carnival to gain <color=#ff9900>Carnival Pts</color>. Points are awarded 
for skill uses and finishing position.
63.111:
As you complete <color=#ff9900>Carnival Races</color>, your <color=#ff9900>Challenge Gauge</color> will fill. 
When full, you can enter a <color=#ff9900>Challenge Race</color>. Challenge Races 
come in <color=#ff9900>VERY HARD and EXTREME</color> difficulties.
63.112:
Your trainee will gain the <color=#ff9900>Carnival Trait</color> when 
finishing top 5 in an event race. When a trainee 
receives this trait, she will gain <color=#ff9900>bonus stats and 
a chance to max motivation</color> in the event races.
63.113:
When a trainee with the <color=#ff9900>Carnival Bonus Skill</color> finishes top 5 
in an event race, the trait's <color=#ff9900>level will go up</color>. The <color=#ff9900>stat bonus 
and max motivation chance will increase</color> with skill level.
63.114:
The <color=#ff9900>Carnival pts</color> obtained in Racing Carnival 
races can be <color=#ff9900>traded for items</color> in the shop!
63.115:
Challenge the daily Legend races! Pieces of 
the challenged Legend trainee can be acquired. 
Tickets will <color=#ff9900>refill to max</color> every day.
63.116:
Create your own <color=#ff9900>Trainer Card</color>, and share it as an image on 
social media. Show your info along with trainee, support and 
<color=#ff9900>customizable graphics</color>.
63.117:
<color=#ff9900>Traits</color> for your Star Trainee can also be <color=#ff9900>shared on 
social media</color>. Show off your glorious trainee on your card!
63.118:
The <color=#ff9900>Snapshot function</color> allows you to take photos of <color=#ff9900>Races, 
Lives, and Stories</color>. Snapshots can be processed and 
edited in a few ways to add your own flair.
63.119:
Saved snapshots are stored in the <color=#ff9900>Photo Library</color> and accessible at 
any time、They can also be used on your <color=#ff9900>Trainer or Club Cards</color>.
63.120:
When entering races, you will <color=#ff9900>obtain Shop Coins</color>! Shop 
Coins can be traded for training items in the special 
shop. Training items can increase stats, recover energy, 
boost training growths, and have <color=#ff9900>various other effects 
that are sure to help your training</color>!
63.121:
For your first training <color=#ff9900>we recommend you play the URA Finale 
scenario</color> to get to know the trainees better! Many things 
happen on the path you both take towards her unique goals. 
Aim to win on the big, dream-like stage of the URA Finale!
63.122:
Each scenario <color=#ff9900>has many exclusive events!</color> We hope you will enjoy 
discovering new aspects of the trainees through their daily 
life and their expressions as they go through the seasons!
63.123:
<color=#ff9900>Everyone's Top Umadol Project</color> is an event where rewards are 
gained for obtaining fans through training. Personal rewards 
are obtained based on total fans gained in training mode.
63.124:
When finishing training in <color=#ff9900>Golshi-chan Mode</color> you 
will gain rewards based on progress. In Golshi-chan 
mode <color=#ff9900>opponents in races</color> will be stronger.
63.125:
Overall rewards are based on <color=#ff9900>total fans gained by all trainers 
in the event</color>. The more fans, the more luxurious the rewards.
63.126:
<color=#ff9900>Room Match</color> allows you to race against other trainers 
with your Veteran Trainees <color=#ff9900>Host</color>: Set your own 
conditions for the race <color=#ff9900>Join</color>: Join another trainer's 
race. <color=#ff9900>Watch</color>: Enter an ID to watch the race.
63.127:
The race held will be G1. There are two modes. <color=#ff9900>Simple Mode</color> 
allows you to hold a race right away. <color=#ff9900>Detailed mode</color> allows you 
to choose weather, participation conditions, etc. first. Up to 
<color=#ff9900>3 trainees per person</color> can be entered.
63.128:
Recruit participants for your races from the participation 
list. Trainers close to you can also be recruited using 
the <color=#ff9900>Club and Online Sharing functions</color>. For future reference, 
<color=#ff9900>you can save your favorite races</color>. Have a go organizing 
a race with your friends!
63.129:
Save trainees in <color=#ff9900>My Entrants</color> to <color=#ff9900>select them easily</color> during 
registration! You can also save them from the results screen.
63.130:
Save trainees in <color=#ff9900>My Entrants</color> to <color=#ff9900>select them easily</color> during 
registration! You can also save them from the results screen.
63.131:
Dress your trainees in your favorite outfits! 
Changing the outfit of a trainee will change it 
for <color=#ff9900>all of her versions</color>. Note that 
in some situations the outfit won't display.
63.132:
You can select <color=#ff9900>Double TP Usage</color> at the start of training to 
<color=#ff9900>double SHOW gauge fill rate</color> at the end.
63.133:
After 4 Promotional Lives, the <color=#ff9900>Grand Live</color> is held! 
Keeping things fresh and <color=#ff9900>learning various tracks</color> 
for each Live will greatly benefit your trainees!
63.134:
Through training, <color=#ff9900>5 different Performances</color> can be earned! 
Which ones go with which training changes every turn.
63.135:
The Performances earned increase with the 
<color=#ff9900>training's level</color> and the <color=#ff9900>amount of trainees participating</color>. 
Friendship training will also provide 2 Performances!
63.136:
The Performances earned can be spend on <color=#ff9900>Lessons</color> to 
gain <color=#ff9900>Tracks or Live Techniques</color>. Both of these are 
highly useful to the <color=#ff9900>development of your trainees</color>.
63.137:
Performances can be spent on <color=#ff9900>Lessons</color> to gain <color=#ff9900>Tracks or 
Live Techniques</color>! Learning these will provide an immediate 
<color=#ff9900>Talent Bonus</color>, and Tracks will additionally provide 
a <color=#ff9900>Live Bonus</color> which will be active from the next Live 
until the end of training.
63.138:
When lacking the Performances for a Lesson, it can be 
<color=#ff9900>reserved</color>. Doing so will display the <color=#ff9900>Performances needed</color> 
to learn it in the Training screens.
63.139:
Each Track learned will increase the <color=#ff9900>Live Anticipation</color>. 
Maxing this gauge will ensure the live's <color=#ff9900>Great Success</color>. 
Anticipation will reset every live, so prepare new Tracks 
for each live!
63.140:
At a <color=#ff9900>Live</color>, the <color=#ff9900>songs learnt will be played</color>. With a specific 
number of new Tracks, the <color=#ff9900>live will be a Great Success</color>. 
Trainees will <color=#ff9900>grow more</color> when the live is a Great Sucess, 
so aim for this using the Anticipation gauge as reference.
63.141:
When new Tracks are played at a Live, their 
<color=#ff9900>Live Bonus</color> will be active from the next turn. 
Similar bonuses will <color=#ff9900>stack</color>.
63.142:
In <color=#ff9901>Transfer Requests: StarHorse4 Edition</color> 
you can transfer your trained trainees to another team 
to receive rewards. Be careful, the transfer is permanent, 
and you can't just call those girls back!
63.143:
The <color=#ff9901>expectations</color> of the receiving teams differ, and based on 
that and the trainee you sent the rewards can change. 
<color=#ff9901>That one trainer</color> seems to have a request of <color=#ff9901>☆4 difficulty</color>!
63.144:
In ☆3 difficulty requests, you will receive <color=#ff9901>pieces of the 
trainee you sent</color>. Moreover, from this difficulty onwards 
you can get <color=#ff9901>Star Fruits</color> exclusive to this <color=#ff9901>StarHorse4 Edition</color>.
63.145:
Take on Scout Races with a team of 7. The Team captain 
will be one of your trained trainees. The others can be 
selected through <color=#ff9900>scouting</color>. Build the strongest team through 
continuous <color=#ff9900>racing and scouting</color>!
63.146:
You can obtain up to 5 <color=#ff9900>Scout Race Tickets</color> every day at 12. 
Daily missions and items can also grant you some. 
<color=#ff9900>Every 3 races</color>, the <color=#ff9900>race details</color> will change. You can check 
this from the Race Schedule.
63.147:
Entering Scout Races will grant <color=#ff9900>Scout Pt</color>. The amount 
will vary based on race result, opponent team rank, and 
any bonuses. Aim to win a variety of different races!
63.148:
During <color=#ff9900>Scout Time</color>, you can <color=#ff9900>scout</color> the opponent's trainees 
and swap around your own team. The <color=#ff9900>Scout Pt</color> needed 
to scout depend on the target trainee's rank.
63.149:
Strengthen your team and <color=#ff9900>increase team rank</color> to 
receive rewards. In addition, clear <color=#ff9900>limited missions</color> 
to gain more rewards!
63.150:
When you <color=#ff9900>win</color> a <color=#ff9900>Scout Race</color>, the team captain and members 
are registered in the <color=#ff9900>Roster</color>, and you will get a bonus to 
the entire team's stats and your Scout Pt. Further, by 
drafting specific combinations of trainees in your team 
you can obtain <color=#ff9900>Team Skills</color> upon victory.
63.151:
The <color=#ff9900>Event Boost</color> lets you use double the TP 
at the start of training to double the amount of <color=#ff9900>Trial Coin</color> received at the end.
63.152:
You can fill the <color=#ff9900>Stroll Gauge</color> by playing <color=#ff9900>various parts of 
the game</color> or through the <color=#ff9900>stroll login bonus</color>. Once the gauge 
is at <color=#ff9900>MAX</color>, you can <color=#ff9900>take a stroll</color> with one of your trainees. 
 
※The gauge will fill from play after using your login bonus.
63.153:
With the Photo Studio you can take <color=#ff9900>photos 
at training, outings</color>, and various other settings.
63.154:
After selecting a trainee to photograph, you can set up 
the <color=#ff9900>seasons, locations, and so on</color>. For <color=#ff9900>training photos</color> 
partners can also be selected.
63.155:
During shooting the action can be <color=#ff9900>paused or looped</color>. 
Find the perfect timing to snap your favourite shots! 
Photos can also be <color=#ff9900>edited with filters and the like</color>.
63.156:
Going for a stroll increases your <color=#ff9900>Stroll Partner's Bond</color>. 
Different <color=#ff9900>destinations</color> will play unique animations and 
provide different <color=#ff9900>rewards</color>. During the campaign you can 
rewatch any animations you've seen by long-clicking 
a trainee.
63.157:
On the KIRARI MAGIC SHOW campaign page you can check 
not only campaign info but from 21 December you can join 
in on the TOKYO MEGA ILLUMI collab event, as well as 
find a gift hiding in the <color=#ff9900>present box once a day</color>.
63.158:
The trainees gather at the Ooi Racecourse for the 
TOKYO MEGA ILLUMI Christmas Live collab. But the 
MEGA TREE's decorations are lagging behind!? It's time 
for the trainees' power to shine☆ With everyone's help 
the MEGA TREE will be glittering in no time!
63.159:
<color=#ff9900>Happy Meek</color> will appear in training! 
Work together with her and aim to enter the URA Finale!
63.160:
Training with her can trigger a <color=#ff9900>Duel</color> around a specific stat 
or energy level! The higher this chosen Duel Subject is, 
the easier it will be to win the Duel, so train well!
63.161:
Should you manage to win a certain number of Duels, 
a <color=#ff9900>stronger Happy Meek</color> will appear in the URA Finale! 
Train your trainee to take victory on the Finals stage!
63.162:
After a team member has had a Spirit Explosion, they can have 
one further <color=#ff9900>EX Spirit Explosion</color>! This will <color=#ff9900>grant them even more 
stats, as will your trainee, who will additionally receive a special 
skill hint</color>! EX Spirit Explosions add even more fire to your training!
63.163:
Under <color=#ff9900>certain conditions</color>, you can face a <color=#ff9900>Powerhouse Team</color> in 
the Aoharu Hai! When winning against this team your trainee 
will grow considerably! <color=#ff9900>Something may happen in the finals</color> too…!?
63.164:
In the TOKYO MEGA ILLUMI collab event, KIRARI MAGIC 
SHOW, you will receive rewards for gathering <color=#ff9900>ornaments 
for the tree</color> from Career and missions. Individual rewards 
will be handed out based on the amount you collect.
63.165:
With the <color=#ff9900>ornaments gathered by all trainers</color> we can make 
the MEGA TREE glitter as it should. Watch the <color=#ff9900>tree change</color> 
on the event homepage and help claim the extravagant 
global event rewards by collecting tons of ornaments!
63.166:
Reach for your trainee's dreams over 3 years 
with the aid of the three goddesses! 
<color=#ff9900>Clear all goals to enter the special Grand Masters race!</color> 
Hold your own against the goddesses and aim for victory!
63.167:
The goddesses will bestow <color=#ff9900>Knowledge Shards</color> upon you 
when training, resting, going out, or racing! These shards 
will grant <color=#ff9900>increased stat gains</color> and other useful effects. 
<color=#ff9900>And friendship training grants 2 shards at once!?</color>
63.168:
Every 2 <color=#ff9900>Knowledge Shards</color> will also grant a <color=#ff9900>Knowledge Crystal</color>! 
These also bestow effects to improve your training. <color=#ff9900>Crystals inherit 
effects from their shards</color>, so pile them up and strengthen your trainee!
63.169:
Shards and Crystals collected show in the Knowledge Tree. 
Reach the end to obtain <color=#ff9900>Goddess' Wisdom</color>! Use this wisdom 
whenever you wish to <color=#ff9900>power up various actions for 1 turn</color>! 
The effects vary per goddess, so check the details in the tree.
63.170:
Before you can enter the Grand Masters, you will face a 
<color=#ff9900>special race at the end of each year</color>! Seize victory in all of 
them with your trainee and the power of the goddesses!
63.171:
The Grand Masters is 1 special race, facing the ultimate rivals in 
the <color=#ff9900>three goddesses</color> themselves! <color=#ff9900>Darley Arabian, Godolphin Barb, 
and Byerley Turk</color> await! Challenge them with your well-trained 
trainee and seize victory!
63.172:
This event is divided in a <color=#ff9901>Main Stage</color> and an <color=#ff9901>Extra Stage</color>. 
In the Main Stage you take on races with a team of three. 
Based on how you do, your <color=#ff9901>League Score</color> will increase, 
and based on this score, your <color=#ff9901>League Rank</color> increases, 
granting various rewards!
63.176:
Fill the research gauge by <color=#ff9900>playing various content</color>. 
When the gauge is filled at least once, return to the event page 
to receive an amount of <color=#ff9900>research rewards</color> equal to gauge fills.
63.177:
<color=#ff9900>The login bloom bonus</color>, <color=#ff9900>completing training</color>, and <color=#ff9900>entering stadium </color> 
will fill your <color=#ff9900>bloom gauge</color>. When the gauge is at <color=#ff9900>MAX</color> you can 
<color=#ff9900>move 1 cell further</color> on the main event page. 
 
※ The gauge will fill from play after using your bloom bonus.
63.178:
Upon moving cells, their <color=#ff9900>local episodes</color> are unlocked, and their 
<color=#ff9900>reward</color> acquired. On 4/2 & 4/3 at 12:00 a <color=#ff9900>new area</color> will unlock, 
allowing you to move into new cells. 
 
※ The bloom gauge won't fill if there are no new cells to move to.
63.179:
During training, fulfilling the conditions for the Rare Skills 
unlocked by awakening a trainee allows you to upgrade 
them to powerful <color=#ff9900>Evolved Skills</color> unique to each trainee 
at the end of training.
63.180:
By spending <color=#ff9900>Skill Points</color>, you can acquire <color=#ff9900>skills</color> that activate 
during races. There are various conditions to activate skills, 
such as the <color=#ff9900>race distance and running strategy</color>, so try to 
<color=#ff9900>acquire skills that match her aptitudes</color>.
63.181:
The <color=#ff9900>hint Lv</color> of a skill may be increased by the result 
of a <color=#ff9900>Support Card or Trainee Event</color>. The skill Pt you 
<color=#ff9900>consume will be discounted</color> depending on the hint Lv, 
so organize support cards that have the skills you want 
to acquire to get the best deals.
63.182:
There are three main types of skills. 
<color=#ff9900>Unique skills</color> are special skills inherent to a trainee. 
<color=#ff9900>Rare skills</color> are powerful skills obtained from special experiences. 
<color=#ff9900>Normal Skills</color> are skills picked up through diverse situations. 
Some other skills can only be acquired through <color=#ff9900>certain Traits</color>.
63.183:
The rare skills trainees gain from awakening and those gained 
from some training scenarios can be <color=#ff9900>evolved</color> at the end of training 
when their respective requirements are fulfilled. Keep an eye on 
them as you train to obtain these <color=#ff9900>Evolved Skills</color>.
63.184:
The <color=#ff9900>Trait Research Reports</color> obtained as rewards for research 
allow you to increase the <color=#ff9900>★</color> of a trainee's <color=#ff9900>trait</color>. 
※ Reports apply to most but not all traits. 
※ Reports don't carry over to next event.
63.185:
Using the <color=#ff9900>Event Boost</color>, twice the TP will be used at the 
start of training to double the <color=#ff9900>Research Gauge's fill rate</color>.
63.186:
Each area's final cell is the <color=#ff9900>destination</color>. 
Reaching it unlocks a <color=#ff9900>special video</color>. 
Videos are available from 4/1～4/3 at 19:00 and 4/4 at 12:00.
63.187:
For a time you can <color=#ff9900>cheer for a trainee by hitting 
the "Neighce!" button</color>! Doing so grants you some 
<color=#ff9900>neighce rewards</color> and can be done once per block (A-B).
63.188:
The Extra Stage has opened! You can watch the main race 
for the A and B blocks and each race of your own team. 
Watching races grants rewards too!
63.189:
Clubs can create their own original <color=#ff9901>Club Cards</color> and share 
them as images <color=#ff9901>online</color>. These are customizable cards 
showing the Club's details and other info.
63.190:
At <color=#ff9900>12am every day</color> you receive <color=#ff9900>5 League Tickets</color>, each of 
which grants entry to <color=#ff9900>5 races</color>. Each race features 5 teams, 
with the weather and going randomized every race. 
No other race conditions will change.
63.191:
With each entered race, your <color=#ff9900>Hero Gauge</color> will fill, 
and when MAXed you gain access to a <color=#ff9900>Hero Skill</color> 
for <color=#ff9900>5 races</color>! Some Hero Skills can be activated 
by using Race Items.
63.192:
From <color=#ff9900>BRONZE1 to PLATINUM4</color>, there are 16 League Ranks. 
Reaching PLATINUM1 bestows a <color=#ff9900>unique title</color> featuring the 
name of one of your entered trainees. Which one can be 
freely chosen from those who meet the requirements. 
Battle varied teams on your way to PLATINUM!
63.193:
The teams whose League Score ranks in the top 96 can 
progress to the Extra Stage! This is composed of 12 races 
per block, for <color=#ff9900>24 races</color> total. Trainees entering these 
races are granted the <color=#ff9900>SUPER HERO</color> epithet! 
※Participation can be set in the options
63.194:
In the Extra Stage you can cheer for a trainee 
by hitting the <color=#ff9900>"Neighce!"</color> button once per race. 
Doing so grants you some neighce rewards.
63.195:
In each block, the <color=#ff9900>11th race is the Main Race</color>. 
In A block this will be raced by the top 4 teams, 
in B block the 5th–8th placed ones. Watching the 
Main Race grants rewards. All 24 races can also 
be watched with the focus on 1 trainee.
63.196:
Through Transfer Requests, you can <color=#ff9901>obtain gifts for transferring 
your trained trainees to another trainer's team</color>. 
Be aware you can't get trainees back once transferred! 
Ads are updated <color=#ff9901>every monday at 5:00</color>.
63.197:
Each team has its own expectations. These not only come with 
<color=#ff9901>different rewards</color>, but <color=#ff9901>the higher the expectations, the more 
luxurious the rewards</color> for transferring your trainee to them.
63.198:
Teams with high expectations will reward a piece of the 
transferred <color=#ff9901>Veteran Trainee</color> as a thank you gift.
63.199:
Only Club Leaders can change the <color=#ff9901>Club Card</color>. 
Club info, comments, and images can be added 
and customized to show the appeal of your Club!
63.200:
Now you can play the crane game from Career 
whenever you like. Aim for your favourite <color=#ff9900>trainee 
plushies (Paka-Puchi)</color> and view them in your collection! 
Catch them all and <color=#ff9900>complete the set</color>!
63.201:
In this scenario trainees replace their individual goals with 
the shared goal of conquering the <color=#ff9900>Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe</color>! 
An opportunity to do so appears <color=#ff9900>once during the classic and 
senior year each</color>. Work hard and chase your dreams with the 
other Project l'Arc members to triumph at the arc!
63.202:
Finishing graded races in the money and SS Matches with l'Arc 
members gets you <color=#ff9900>Supporter Pt</color> and increases <color=#ff9900>Fan Expectations</color>. 
Increased Expectations confers stat growth, a training bonus, 
and facility level ups to lead to further growth for your trainee!
63.203:
To unleash their true ability on foreign tracks it will 
be necessary to <color=#ff9900>gain or upgrade Foreign Aptitudes</color>. 
Obtain Foreign Aptitudes by fulfilling their requirements 
and level them up to <color=#ff9900>overcome challenges at Foreign Races</color>!
63.204:
<color=#ff9900>Interleague friendlies with foreign trainees</color> using VR at a virtual 
Longchamps are held 4 times during training. Like Foreign Races, 
these races <color=#ff9900>have challenges</color>. Supporter and Apt. Pt can be gained 
from them, so <color=#ff9900>raise those Foreign Aptitudes</color> and aim for victory!
63.205:
You can <color=#ff9900>gain Foreign Aptitudes</color> through SS Matches 
and winning races. <color=#ff9900>The requirements for each vary</color> 
so check them out to gain as many as you can!
63.206:
The <color=#ff9900>Apt. Pt</color> gained through SS Matches and expeditions 
abroad can be used to <color=#ff9900>level up Foreign Aptitudes</color>! 
Leveling them up not only helps you overcome challenges 
but grants positive effects to aptitudes and bonuses in training. 
Gather Apt. Pt and level up aggressively!
63.207:
l'Arc members fill the <color=#ff9900>Star Gauge</color> through training and races, 
and <color=#ff9900>once it's full they can join an SS Match</color>! SS Matches will 
take up a turn, but can be held with <color=#ff9900>up to 5 participants</color>, granting 
Supporter Pt and stats! You might also find yourself in an even 
more powerful <color=#ff9900>SSS Match</color> at times.
63.208:
When winning an SS Match with l'Arc members, each member 
will <color=#ff9900>get a winning bonus</color>. Rewards are varied, including Skill Hints 
and Conditions, so take on the challenge of SS Matches once 
you get a few members together!
63.209:
From early July to early October an <color=#ff9900>Expedition Abroad</color> 
will have you undergo <color=#ff9900>special training</color> in France. 
<color=#ff9900>You can gain tons of Apt. Pt</color> while abroad so it is 
a great chance to level up Foreign Aptitudes!
63.210:
During the Expedition Abroad you will enter the <color=#ff9900>Prix Niel</color> 
and the <color=#ff9900>Prix Foy</color> in early September of the classic 
and senior years, respectively. These serve as trials 
for the <color=#ff9900>Prix de l'Arc de Triomphe</color> in early October!
63.211:
<color=#ff9900>Once per day</color> you can enter a <color=#ff9900>special race</color> after 10 scout races. 
These special races don't consume a ticket and are raced against 
special NPC teams. Winning this race earns you a large amount 
of Scout Pts.
63.212:
In the <color=#ff9901>Derby Stallion: Masters edition of Transfers</color> 
you can transfer your trained trainees to another trainer's 
team for rewards. Be aware that once you transfer someone, 
she will be gone and you will not be able to get her back.
63.213:
Each team has its own expectations. These not only come with 
<color=#ff9901>different rewards</color>, but <color=#ff9901>the higher the expectations, the more 
luxurious the rewards</color> for transferring your trainee to them. 
This time, even more luxurious <color=#ff9901>grade 4</color> rewards are available!
63.214:
In the <color=#ff9901>Derby Stallion: Masters edition of Transfer Requests</color> 
all transfers will also reward <color=#ff9901>pieces of the transferred trainee</color>. 
Gather a lot of pieces to develop your trainees!
63.215:
Each race comes in a number of LEVELs. 
When winning a race, the <color=#ff9901>next LEVEL</color> will unlock.
63.216:
You will get a reward the <color=#ff9901>first time you finish first</color> in each LEVEL. 
Work hard, aim high!
63.217:
Each Veteran Trainee can only 
<color=#ff9901>participate three times</color>.
63.218:
<color=#ff9900>Training Feats</color> can be reached from stats and skill points 
gained during training. Most feats will reflect new gains 
as you progress through training. Aim for high stats and 
skill points to reach bigger Training Feats!
63.219:
You will gain rewards based on your training feats during 
the tallying period. When you buy a <color=#ff9900>Premium Pass</color>, you will 
also get additional premium rewards. Keep on training 
to receive a ton of rewards!
63.220:
The Monthly Match is comprised of 5 <color=#ff9900>Regular Races</color> with 
different conditions. Regular Races can be entered by using <color=#ff9900>RP</color>. 
Conditions for each Regular Race change every week.
63.221:
Each of the 5 races comes with <color=#ff9900>Classes</color>. 
The furthest Class you reach is referred to as the <color=#ff9900>Highest Class</color>. 
Increase your Highest Class to gain <color=#ff9900>rewards</color>!
63.222:
You can rise through Classes by gaining enough <color=#ff9900>Class Pts</color>. 
These can be gained in <color=#ff9900>Regular Races</color> and are affected by 
finishing position, opponent strength, etc.
63.223:
After <color=#ff9900>Class 8</color>, you will be reset to <color=#ff9900>Class 7</color> when the week rolls over. 
You will then be able to gain <color=#ff9900>rewards</color> for reaching <color=#ff9900>Class 8–10</color> again.
63.224:
At 05:00 every saturday the <color=#ff9900>Weekend Challenge</color> starts. 
During this time, you can gain <color=#ff9900>Weekend Pts</color> from Regular Races 
and obtain <color=#ff9900>weekend rewards</color> by increasing your Highest Class.
63.225:
On the last Saturday of the Monthly Race period a <color=#ff9900>Final Race</color> 
will be held. This allows you to <color=#ff9900>race once in the highest Class</color> 
and gain <color=#ff9900>rewards</color> based on your finishing position.
63.226:
The <color=#ff9900>U.A.F.</color> is an athletic festival highlighting trainees' 
physical abilities. Five competitions are held over a period of 
three years. Developing a high level in each discipline is 
the key to doing well. Master as many disciplines as possible 
and aim to emerge as the festival's <color=#ff9900>overall victor</color>!
63.227:
New trainings incorporating sports <color=#ff9900>disciplines</color> have been developed 
with an eye on competing in the U.A.F. competitions. There are 15 
disciplines in total, and each turn 5 will be chosen at random for 
training, for which the corresponding <color=#ff9900>discipline levels</color> will increase.
63.228:
The 15 disciplines are split into <color=#ff9900>3 categories</color>. When training any 
discipline, other disciplines of the same category will provide 
<color=#ff9900>Link Training</color>. This causes all relevant discipline levels to increase, 
and also provides a boost to the training's effectiveness.
63.229:
When the combined discipline level of a category reaches 
a given value, that category will <color=#ff9900>Heat Up</color> your trainee's passion. 
This grants an effect unique to the category, improving training 
for a number of turns. Use it efficiently to make big gains!
63.230:
With enough discipline levels, <color=#ff9900>hype for the competitions</color> will rise. 
Entering with maximum hype improves results, leading to greater 
growth for your trainee. Each competition grants a permanent 
bonus to training based on the number of disciplines in which she 
was victorious, so aim to win as many as you can!
63.231:
You can change your training disciplines by consulting with 
Sonan Elfie! You can do this a set number of times for each 
competition and the URA Finale. Consultations won't carry 
over between them however, and you will lose unused ones. 
Don't be afraid to consult and set up Link Trainings!
63.232:
Your trainee will compete in all 15 disciplines during 
the competitions. Winning enough of them will make her 
the overall victor and lead to greater growth. You will also 
receive a permanent bonus to training based on amount 
of disciplines won.
63.233:
You can change your training disciplines by consulting with 
Sonan Elfie! You can do this a set number of times for each 
competition and the URA Finale. Consultations won't carry 
over between them however, and you will lose unused ones. 
Don't be afraid to consult and set up Link Trainings!
63.234: Trainees expend <color=#ff9900>energy</color> to <color=#ff9900>train</color> and increase their <color=#ff9900>stats</color>.
63.235:
Training with trainees organized as a <color=#ff9900>support card</color> will 
increase your <color=#ff9900>training efficiency and Friendship Gauge</color>. When the 
Friendship Gauge is full, <color=#ff9900>Friendship Training</color> can occur. You may <color=#ff9900>gain 
various benefits</color> if certain others watch you train as well.
63.236:
When a support card trainee has a <color=#ff9900>! mark</color> on her, grab your 
chance to <color=#ff9900>increase Hint Lv of one of this partner's skills</color> upon 
successfully completing the training session. This will confer 
a <color=#ff9900>discount on the Skill Pt needed to acquire the skill</color>.
63.237:
If you train your trainee when her <color=#ff9900>energy is low</color>, she may 
<color=#ff9900>fail the training</color> and her ability may not improve. There are 
<color=#ff9900>various disadvantages</color> to failing a training session, so always 
keep an eye on her <color=#ff9900>energy and failure rate</color>!
63.238:
During the Monthly Match period you can <color=#ff9900>rent a support card</color> 
not only from friends, but also <color=#ff9900>from the provided lineup</color>. 
You can do this 3 times per day, resetting at 05:00.
63.239:
During the Monthly Match period you can <color=#ff9900>rent a support card</color> 
not only from friends, but also <color=#ff9900>from the provided lineup</color>. 
You can do this 3 times per day, resetting at 05:00.
63.240:
<color=#ff9900>The Great Food Festival</color> is all about letting your trainee flex 
her cooking skills. Over 3 years, 4 Tasting Parties will be held, 
culminating in the grand festival itself! Polish those culinary 
talents, whipping up and enjoying a multitude of dishes, to <color=#ff9900>greatly 
satisfy</color> the participating trainees in the cooking challenges!
63.241:
You gain <color=#ff9900>Cooking Points</color> by cooking dishes. Reaching the specified 
point goals for each <color=#ff9900>Cooking Challenge</color> will lead to greater growth! 
Cooking points will furthermore gain you training bonuses, 
increased specialty rates, and other great effects!
63.242:
Every 4 turns you can harvest the 5 kinds of produce and gain 
<color=#ff9900>Farm Points</color>! You receive <color=#ff9900>Cultivation Stamps</color> from actions like 
training, resting, going out, etc, which will increase the amount 
of corresponding produce harvested! <color=#ff9900>Really get your hands dirty</color> 
to get special stamps, mainly from friendship training, which 
increase harvest for all produce!
63.243:
Each turn you can choose to cook 1 dish with your harvested 
produce, providing a <color=#ff9900>training bonus</color> to a specific type of training! 
There are also some dishes which provide <color=#ff9900>extra stats from races, 
recover energy</color>, and so on!
63.244:
Reaching the point goals for each Cooking Challenge will leave 
the trainees <color=#ff9900>Fully Satisfied</color>, boost your trainee's growth, 
and increase her cooking's effects! Furthermore, <color=#ff9900>some Tasting 
Parties</color> will unlock <color=#ff9900>new dishes</color> afterwards, <color=#ff9900>further increasing 
your farm's produce levels</color>!
63.245:
During the summer camp, other students will help you 
take care of your farming field. <color=#ff9900>Produce will be shipped 
to the camp site</color>, but since you can't go to your farm, 
<color=#ff9900>you can't increase produce level</color>! In return for <color=#ff9900>receiving 
produce every turn</color> while at camp, you will only receive 
<color=#ff9900>half the amount and Farm Points</color>!
63.246:
During the summer camp, other students will help you 
take care of your farming field. <color=#ff9900>Produce will be shipped 
to the camp site</color>, but since you can't go to your farm, 
<color=#ff9900>you can't increase produce level</color>! In return for <color=#ff9900>receiving 
produce every turn</color> while at camp, you will only receive 
<color=#ff9900>half the amount and Farm Points</color>!
63.247:
The <color=#ff9900>Farm Points</color> you receive along with a harvest can be used to 
increase a specific produce's <color=#ff9900>Produce Level</color>. Doing so not only 
<color=#ff9900>increases the amounts harvested</color>, but <color=#ff9900>improves some dishes</color> 
and can even <color=#ff9900>unlock new effects</color>!
63.248:
The Produce Levels of your farm are limited. <color=#ff9900>Some Tasting Parties</color> 
will <color=#ff9900>increase this limit</color>! Higher levels require more Farm Points, 
but improve things like the amount harvested, so try to aim for 
as high a level as you can!
63.249:
<color=#ff9900>Room Match</color> allows you to race against other trainers 
with your Veteran Trainees <color=#ff9900>Host</color>: Set your own 
conditions for the race <color=#ff9900>Join</color>: Join another trainer's 
race. <color=#ff9900>Watch</color>: Enter an ID to watch the race.
63.250:
The race held will be G1. There are two modes. <color=#ff9900>Simple Mode</color> 
allows you to hold a race right away. <color=#ff9900>Detailed mode</color> allows you 
to choose weather, participation conditions, etc. first. Up to 
<color=#ff9900>3 trainees per person</color> can be entered.
63.251:
Recruit participants for your races from the participation 
list. Trainers close to you can also be recruited using 
the <color=#ff9900>Club and Online Sharing functions</color>. For future reference, 
<color=#ff9900>you can save your favorite races</color>. Have a go organizing 
a race with your friends!
63.252:
Save trainees in <color=#ff9900>My Entrants</color> to <color=#ff9900>select them easily</color> during 
registration! You can also save them from the results screen.
63.254:
Trainer information can be viewed and set here. You can expect
special comments from the active home screen trainee on your
<color=#ff9900>birthday</color>, and some scenario content might change depending on
your <color=#ff9900>gender</color>. (Gender can be changed at any time)
63.255:
You can set up a <color=#ff9900>Star Trainee</color> and <color=#ff9900>support card</color> to 
help your followers in their training. You will earn <color=#ff9900>Friend Pt 
the next day</color> depending on the number of times you do this. 
(This will be given as a gift)
63.256:
You can select the Star Trainee and support 
card of the friends you <color=#ff9900>follow</color> from each list in training. 
You also earn more Friend Pt by helping a <color=#ff9900>mutual follower</color> 
to train. If you don't have many followers, try looking 
in the <color=#ff9900>recommendations</color>.
63.257:
You can check the items you currently have. You can also use 
consumable items. Some items can be converted to Money. 
<color=#ff9900>Converting items cannot be undone</color>, so be careful.
63.265:
Using TP, you can <color=#ff9900>advance the time remaining</color> on an event. 
<color=#ff9900>TP will not be refunded</color>, so choose your timing carefully.
63.266: The <color=#ff9900>Dream Fest</color> is a new race, and the result of the legendary trainees' desire to re-energize the racing scene. It is held at the end of every year, with many participants. Power up by training under these legend's guidance and aim for victory!
63.267: Legendary trainees will support your training! Various actions will cause each of their <color=#ff9900>coach gauges</color> to rise, with friendship training being especially effective. Which action contributes to which gauge changes per turn, so juggle them to your needs!
63.268: <color=#ff9900>Teachings</color> are acquired through a legendary trainee's coaching and are key to improving. <color=#ff9900>One</color> teaching can be acquired every 6 turns by selecting it from the options presented. These options differ based on the coach gauge's state. Make good use of them for effective training!
63.269: <color=#ff9900>Guidance</color> is something like a special power provided by the legendary trainees, with each legend's having its own unique features. It can be provided only once and is based on the teachings acquired. Combine both for maximum training gains!
63.270: <color=#ff9900>Teachings</color> are acquired through a legendary trainee's coaching and are key to improving. <color=#ff9900>One</color> teaching can be acquired every 6 turns by selecting it from the options presented. These options differ based on the coach gauge's state. Make good use of them for effective training!
63.271: The <color=#ff9900>Dream Fest</color> is a new race, and the result of the legendary trainees' desire to re-energize the racing scene. It is held at the end of every year, with many participants. Power up by training under these legend's guidance and aim for victory!
63.272: <color=#ff9900>Guidance</color> is something like a special power provided by the legendary trainees, with each legend's having its own unique features. It can be provided only once and is based on the teachings acquired. Combine both for maximum training gains!
63.273: The <color=#ff9900>Training Guide</color> functionality <color=#ff9900>assists you</color> in entering your trainee in current or upcoming <color=#ff9900>racing events</color>. Select your targets and activate its guidance!
63.274: <color=#ff9900>Set up</color> your target races and running style in the guide to <color=#ff9900>show recommended</color> support cards and skills at the start of training. You can change or cancel your targets at any time.
63.275: <color=#ff9900>Rental Decks</color> are premade decks for use in Career. There is a deck for general use, and ones for sprint, mile, medium, and long races, so you can pick one that fits your trainee. Cards featuring your trainee will automatically be swapped.
63.276: This scenario provides <color=#ff9900>Autoplay</color>. This can be accessed through the button on the main training screen. Training will happen automatically, according to the selected plan, which you can also edit. Make use of autoplay for a more hands-off experience!
63.277: This scenario provides <color=#ff9900>Autoplay</color>. This can be accessed through the button on the main training screen. Training will happen automatically, according to the selected plan, which you can also edit. Make use of autoplay for a more hands-off experience!
63.278: In exchange for great effects, teachings come with conditions. <color=#ff9900>At most 10</color> teachings can be held at any time. When acquiring new teachings beyond this limit, you have the option to <color=#ff9900>swap</color> them with existing ones. Find the best combination for every situation!
63.279: The <color=#ff9900>Uninhabited Island Project</color> aims to develop <color=#ff9900>Tracen Island</color>, constructing the greatest training facilities there. Make use of the special <color=#ff9900>Island Training</color> available from these facilities, and demonstrate the benefits of the project!
63.280: Draft up 5 <color=#ff9900>Construction Plans</color> to complete by the half-yearly Evaluation Meetings! For each, you will need to choose which of the 6 training facilities to focus on. Facility <color=#ff9900>type and construction order</color> will have an effect on island training, so plan well!
63.281: During your career, training and racing will grant <color=#ff9900>Development Pt</color>. These points will feed construction progress, and at certain amounts they will provide benefits like <color=#ff9900>training bonuses and Island Training tickets</color>. Get as many as you can!
63.282: <color=#ff9900>Island Training Tickets</color> let you hold special training sessions at constructed facilities, which will greatly boost your trainee's growth! This <color=#ff9900>Island Training</color>'s effects vary per facility, so plan your island development well!
63.283: This scenario provides <color=#ff9900>Autoplay</color>. This can be accessed through the button on the main career screen. Training will happen automatically, according to the selected plan, which you can also edit. Make use of autoplay for a more hands-off experience!
63.284: Draft up 5 <color=#ff9900>Construction Plans</color> to complete by the half-yearly Evaluation Meetings! For each, you will need to choose which of the 6 training facilities to focus on. Facility <color=#ff9900>type and construction order</color> will have an effect on island training, so plan well!
63.285: Each facility has a level and cost associated. Higher levels will increase its effects, but its costs will rise too! At set levels, you will also have to choose which path to follow further. Keep costs and effects in mind when selecting!
63.286: The summer camp is now an island camp, utilizing the facilities you've built for specialized gains! However, Island Training Tickets can't be used. Tickets obtained and facilities built during the camp will be available after its conclusion.
63.287: Every half a year, the island's development progress is reported on at an <color=#ff9900>Evaluation Meeting</color>, where sponsors will check over the construction work. Greater progress will impress them more! Their reception directly influences extra support to training efficiency, Developtment Pt, and so on!
63.288: All construction has finished!  Development Pt will no longer be gained. You've been given 2 special Island Training Tickets! These expire at the end of the senior year, so look for an opportunity to use them!
63.289: The Uma Jukebox lets you <color=#ff9900>change the background music</color> to any song you've unlocked. Select the jukebox from the menu on the right side of the screen to play songs anywhere you like, whether it's the Home screen, a Career scenario, or any other part of the game.
63.290: The <color=#ff9900>Island Training Tickets</color> obtained at certain Development Pt thresholds let you hold special training sessions at constructed facilities, which will greatly boost your trainee's growth! This <color=#ff9900>Island Training</color>'s effects vary per facility, so plan your island development well!
63.291: You can only hold 1 Island Training Ticket from Development Pt at any time. It is possible to keep them through Evaluation Meetings, but newly gained tickets will overwrite older ones, so use them regularly!
63.293: Once a day, logging in or completing a Career will each grant a <color=#ff9900>Strolling Mood </color> item. You can <color=#ff9900>hold up to 10</color> of these in your inventory. Each item allows you to go on one stroll with a trainee.
63.294:
Going for a stroll increases your <color=#ff9900>Stroll Partner's Bond</color>.
Different <color=#ff9900>destinations</color> will play unique animations.
During the campaign, you can rewatch any unlocked 
animations by tapping a trainee.
63.295:
<size=26>You can watch live performances of <color=#ff9900>songs</color> you have acquired. 
Choose main and back dancers from obtained trainees 
and acquired costumes to watch the live with your favorite 
combination. Songs marked with <color=#ff9900>"Singing"</color>, are voiced 
by the selected trainee. Songs can be acquired through 
the main story and training.</size>
63.296:
In concerts where the <color=#ff9900>Trainer Camera</color> is available, you can
<color=#ff9900>freely position</color> the camera within a set region. There are two
control schemes to choose from: <color=#ff9900>Swipe</color> and <color=#ff9900>Gyro</color>.
Experience concerts in a whole new way!
※ Gyro is only available on the mobile versions.
63.299:
<size=26>You can watch live performances of <color=#ff9900>songs</color> you have acquired. 
Choose main and back dancers from obtained trainees 
and acquired costumes to watch the concert with your favorite 
combination. Songs marked with <color=#ff9900>"Singing"</color>, are voiced 
by the selected trainee. Songs can be acquired through 
the main story and training.</size>
63.300:
In concerts where the <color=#ff9900>Trainer Camera</color> is available, you can
<color=#ff9900>freely position</color> the camera within a set region. There are two
control schemes to choose from: <color=#ff9900>Swipe</color> and <color=#ff9900>Gyro</color>.
Experience concerts in a whole new way!
※ Gyro is only available on the mobile versions.
63.301:
Drag any part of the screen to move the camera, and use
the mouse wheel to zoom in. The <color=#ff9900>reset button</color> at the bottom
left returns the camera to its starting position.
63.302:
In concerts where the <color=#ff9900>Trainer Camera</color> is available, you can
<color=#ff9900>freely position</color> the camera within a set region. There are two
control schemes to choose from: <color=#ff9900>Swipe</color> and <color=#ff9900>Gyro</color>.
Experience concerts in a whole new way!
※ Gyro is only available on the mobile versions.
63.305:
In concerts where the <color=#ff9900>Trainer Camera</color> is available, you can
<color=#ff9900>freely position</color> the camera within a set region. There are two
control schemes to choose from: <color=#ff9900>Swipe</color> and <color=#ff9900>Gyro</color>.
